steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows the word thus, which signals a direct conclusion from the preceding sentence. That sentence states a 2D photograph cannot capture how a building interacts with its surroundings — whether it complements, blends in with, or clashes with nearby sights and activities. The blank must be the logical result of that specific limitation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — serves to exaggerate the building's historical importance.: Historical importance is never mentioned in the passage; this introduces a new topic. ✗
  • Choice B — fails to convey the full impact of the building's design and structure.: If a photograph can’t show how the building interacts with surroundings, it logically cannot convey the building’s full impact — that missing context is part of what makes design meaningful. ✓
  • Choice C — misrepresents how the building is perceived by people who have never visited Beijing.: Too strong — the passage says the photograph can’t capture certain things (omission), not that it misrepresents them (distortion). ✗
  • Choice D — conceals the subtle deterioration of the building's materials.: Material deterioration is never raised; this is unrelated to the passage’s point about surroundings. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
